ddls:
CREATE TABLE "addresses" (
  "address_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each address */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the address */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the address */,
  "street_name" TEXT /* Name of the street */,
  "house_number" INTEGER /* House number of the address */,
  "artery" TEXT /* Artery of the address (e.g., AV, ST) */,
  "borough" TEXT /* Borough where the address is located (e.g., Manhattan, Brooklyn) */,
  "address_type_id" INTEGER /* Type of address (e.g., Residential, Commercial) */,
  "neighborhood_id" INTEGER /* ID of the neighborhood where the address is located */,
  "cross_street_1" TEXT /* Name of the first cross street */,
  "cross_street_2" TEXT /* Name of the second cross street */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* ZIP code of the address */,
  "unit_number" TEXT /* Unit number (e.g., apartment number) */,
  "building_name" TEXT /* Name of the building (if applicable) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the address is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("address_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_addresses_street_name FOREIGN KEY ("street_name") REFERENCES streets ("street_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_addresses_artery FOREIGN KEY ("artery") REFERENCES arteries ("artery_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_addresses_address_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("address_type_id") REFERENCES address_types ("address_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_addresses_borough FOREIGN KEY ("borough") REFERENCES boroughs ("borough_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_addresses_neighborhood_id FOREIGN KEY ("neighborhood_id") REFERENCES neighborhoods ("neighborhood_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "streets" (
  "street_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each street */,
  "street_name" TEXT /* Name of the street */,
  "borough" TEXT /* Borough where the street is located */,
  "neighborhood_id" INTEGER /* ID of the neighborhood where the street is primarily located */,
  "street_classification" TEXT /* Classification of the street (e.g., arterial, local, collector) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("street_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_streets_neighborhood_id FOREIGN KEY ("neighborhood_id") REFERENCES neighborhoods ("neighborhood_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "boroughs" (
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each borough */,
  "borough_name" TEXT /* Name of the borough */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the borough */,
  "area_sq_km" REAL /* Area of the borough in square kilometers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("borough_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "neighborhoods" (
  "neighborhood_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each neighborhood */,
  "neighborhood_name" TEXT /* Name of the neighborhood */,
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* ID of the borough where the neighborhood is located */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the neighborhood */,
  "area_sq_km" REAL /* Area of the neighborhood in square kilometers */,
  "median_income" INTEGER /* Median income of the neighborhood */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("neighborhood_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_neighborhoods_borough_id FOREIGN KEY ("borough_id") REFERENCES boroughs ("borough_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "arteries" (
  "artery_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each artery */,
  "artery_name" TEXT /* Name of the artery (e.g., AV, ST) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the artery */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("artery_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "address_types" (
  "address_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each address type */,
  "address_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the address type (e.g., Residential, Commercial) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the address type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("address_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "address_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history entry */,
  "address_id" INTEGER /* ID of the address being updated */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date of the change */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., update, deletion) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "change_details" TEXT /* Detailed description of the change made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_address_history_address_id FOREIGN KEY ("address_id") REFERENCES addresses ("address_id")
)
column_names: Artery of the address (e.g., AV, ST)
Borough where the address is located (e.g., Manhattan, Brooklyn)
ID of the neighborhood where the address is located
